title: Pushpavaneswarar temple
tagline: Pushpavaneswarar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Pushpavaneswarar temple (also called Poovananathar temple or Thirpoovanam temple) in Thirupuvanam, a town in Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicate
overview.description: Pushpavaneswarar temple (also called Poovananathar temple or Thirpoovanam temple) in Thirupuvanam, a town in Sivaganga district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the Cholas period in the 7th century. Shiva is worshipped as Pushpavaneswarar and his consort Parvathi as Soundaranayagi. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanmars and classified as Paadal Pet

topPlaces.name: Madapuram
topPlaces.description: Madurapuram is a small village in India that lies on the banks of the Vaigai River and on the National Highway 49 that connects Madurai with Rameshwaram. The village is situated 18 kilometres (11 mi) from Madurai.

topPlaces.name: Thirupuvanam, Sivaganga
topPlaces.description: Thiruppuvanam is a town in Manamadurai Assembly constituency and also Manamadurai division in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple
topPlaces.description: Piramanoor Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple is an ancient Shiva temple(now under renovation) in Piramanoor, a village in Tiruppuvanam Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India. Towns Near By Tiruppuvanam (5 km), Madurai (23 km), Sivaganga (24.3 km), Manamadurai(25.2 km).

topPlaces.name: Karseri
topPlaces.description: Karseri is a small village near Sakkimangalam in the Madurai West union of Madurai District, India. It is a small village, famous for the grave of Syed Ibrahim Shaheed who was a soldier in the army of Qutb Sulthan Syed Ibrahim Shaheed of Erwadi and Sulthan Sikandhar Badusha of Thiruparankundram in Madurai.

topPlaces.name: Pallichandai Silaiman
topPlaces.description: Palli Chandai is a small village near Keeladi the famous archeological site of Tamil Nadu on the border of Madurai and Sivaganga districts. The grave of Saalaar Shah Shaheed who was a soldier in the army of Qutb Sulthan Syed Ibrahim Shaheed of Erwadi and Sulthan Sikandhar Badusha of Thiruparankundram in Madurai is located here.

topPlaces.name: Silaiman
topPlaces.description: Silaiman is a panchayat village in Tirupparankunram Mandal (Thiruparankundram) of Madurai District in the Tamil-Nadu state of India. Silaiman is located on the right (south) bank of the Vaigai River, on Route 85 about eight kilometers by road southeast of the city of Madurai, the district headquarters.

topPlaces.name: Keezhadi
topPlaces.description: Keezhadi, also spelt Keeladi (Tamil: [kiːɻaɖi], ISO: Kīḻaṭi), is a village near the village of Silaiman, under the control of Manamadurai Municipalilty, situated on the border between Madurai and Sivagangai districts, in Tamil Nadu, India. The Keezhadi excavation site is located in this area.

topPlaces.name: Keezhadi excavation site
topPlaces.description: Keezhadi, or Keeladi (Tamil: [kiːɻaɖi], ISO: Kīḻaṭi), is a Sangam period settlement site, where excavation is being carried out by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and the Tamil Nadu State Department of Archaeology. This site is located near the town of Keezhadi in Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, about 12 km southeast of Madurai.

items.note: Madurapuram is a small village in India that lies on the banks of the Vaigai River and on the National Highway 49 that connects Madurai with Rameshwaram. The village is situated 18 kilometres (11 mi) from Madurai.
Thiruppuvanam is a town in Manamadurai Assembly constituency and also Manamadurai division in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Piramanoor Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple is an ancient Shiva temple(now under renovation) in Piramanoor, a village in Tiruppuvanam Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India. Towns Near By Tiruppuvanam (5 km), Madurai (23 km), Sivaganga (24.3 km), Manamadurai(25.2 km).

Karseri is a small village near Sakkimangalam in the Madurai West union of Madurai District, India. It is a small village, famous for the grave of Syed Ibrahim Shaheed who was a soldier in the army of Qutb Sulthan Syed Ibrahim Shaheed of Erwadi and Sulthan Sikandhar Badusha of Thiruparankundram in Madurai.

Palli Chandai is a small village near Keeladi the famous archeological site of Tamil Nadu on the border of Madurai and Sivaganga districts. The grave of Saalaar Shah Shaheed who was a soldier in the army of Qutb Sulthan Syed Ibrahim Shaheed of Erwadi and Sulthan Sikandhar Badusha of Thiruparankundram in Madurai is located here.

Silaiman is a panchayat village in Tirupparankunram Mandal (Thiruparankundram) of Madurai District in the Tamil-Nadu state of India. Silaiman is located on the right (south) bank of the Vaigai River, on Route 85 about eight kilometers by road southeast of the city of Madurai, the district headquarters.

Keezhadi, also spelt Keeladi (Tamil: [kiːɻaɖi], ISO: Kīḻaṭi), is a village near the village of Silaiman, under the control of Manamadurai Municipalilty, situated on the border between Madurai and Sivagangai districts, in Tamil Nadu, India. The Keezhadi excavation site is located in this area.

Keezhadi, or Keeladi (Tamil: [kiːɻaɖi], ISO: Kīḻaṭi), is a Sangam period settlement site, where excavation is being carried out by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and the Tamil Nadu State Department of Archaeology. This site is located near the town of Keezhadi in Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, about 12 km southeast of Madurai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pushpavaneswarar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pushpavaneswarar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pushpavaneswarar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (18 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pushpavaneswarar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pushpavaneswarar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pushpavaneswarar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pushpavaneswarar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pushpavaneswarar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Madapuram, Thirupuvanam, Sivaganga, Shree Kailasha Nathar Temple, Karseri, Pallichandai Silaiman.
